questions.question: 1. Which is the largest chain amino acid?

explanations.a: Correct. Tryptophan has the largest side chain among the given amino acids due to its indole ring.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Cysteine contains a thiol group but has a smaller side chain.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Lysine has a long side chain but is smaller than tryptophan.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Aspartic acid has a relatively small acidic side chain.

questions.question: 2. Which of the following amino acids is the smallest of all amino acids?

explanations.a: Correct. Glycine has a hydrogen atom as its side chain, making it the smallest amino acid.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Valine has a branched side chain.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Alanine contains a methyl group.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Serine contains a hydroxymethyl group.

questions.question: 3. Which amino acid can react with itself to form a dimer by formation of a disulfide bond?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Glutamic acid does not contain a sulfhydryl group.
explanations.b: Correct. Two cysteine molecules can form a disulfide bond to produce cystine.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Aspartic acid cannot form disulfide bonds.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Methionine contains sulfur but cannot form disulfide bonds.

explanations.b: Correct. Methionine is a sulfur-containing amino acid.

questions.question: 5. Which amino acid has two chiral centres?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Leucine has only one chiral center.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Lysine has only one chiral center.
explanations.c: Correct. Isoleucine contains two chiral centers.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Cysteine has one chiral center.
